text: Where by an Act of Parliament a wharfage rate is imposed on all except those described by names, coming from abroad and landed at a and, in an action to recover the rate on certain goods, it is alleged by t fendant that they come within the exception, the burden of proving th allegation is upon the defendant. If the particular name has a well known original meaning, and thi dant wishes to prove that that meaning has been extended so as to i the goods upon which the rate is sought to be recovered, he must prove t before the Act was passed, goods of that kind had been imported, that per knowing the nature of those goods had called them by that name, and by their so doing, the goods had become commonly known by that name, Held, therefore, in the absence of such proof, that limestone rock phatized by contact with the droppings of birds, is not " guano" within meaning of sec. 110 of the Melbourne Harbour Trust Act 1890. The words " goods not otherwise enumerated" in the Seventh Sched that Act mean 'goods not otherwise enumerated in any other of the of articles contained in the Schedule," and are not confined to ejusdem generis with those goods the names of which they follow.
citation: Cuming Smith and Company Pty Ltd v Melbourne Harbour Trust Commissioners [1905] HCA 27

text: Under sec. 110 of that Act and the Seventh Schedule to it, the rate ch alteration in the rate on that particular item is made by the Commis Decision of the Supreme Court affirmed. AppeaL from the Supreme Court. * Sec. 110 of the Melbourne Harbour Trust Act 1890 is as follows :— "110, It shall be lawful for the Commissioners to demand collect and receive in respect of all goods merchandise and things whatsoever except goods belonging to Her Majesty's Government passengers' Inggage guano bones bone-dust and live stock and goods arriving coastwise from any place within Victoria landed from any vessel at any wharf dock pier jetty landing stage slip or platform within the port the tolls and rates to be from time to time determined by regulation under this Act; 'and until such regulation be made the tolls and rates to be demanded collected and received shall be the wharfage rates contained in the Seventh Schedule co this Act," &c. + The Seventh Schedule is as follows :— "Liquids in bulk— s. a. Tun or butt each 3 0 Pipe or puncheon Reet ii Hogshead Barrel or quarter-cask Octave keg drum tin jar or other small single package " Other goods— Case crate cask bale box bundle trunk bag keg firkin or package measurin, 30 ate feet and upwards 20 4, " "3 " " non Less than 1 foot Steam boilers millstones chains machinery railway pig iron cordage oakum flax or other fibrous mi carriages furniture and goods not otherwise enumerated, &e., &e. per ton OF AUSTRALIA. } action was brought in the Supreme Court of Victoria by Melbourne Harbour Trust Commissioners against Cuming & Company Proprietary Limited, claiming £300 for e rates at 3s. per ton on 2,000 tons of rock phosphate din Melbourne in May, 1901, ex "Emma." On a summons irections it was ordered that the parties should proceed to ial of the questions agreed upon between them, viz.:—(1) Are goods referred to in the endorsement on the writ of summons "guano" within the meaning of sec. 110 of the Melbourne wbour Trust Act 1890? (2) Are the plaintiffs entitled to and, if so, to what sum for wharfage rates upon the said At the hearing of the action before Hood J. it was proved that ations were made by the Melbourne Harbour Trust Commers in 1900 providing that :—« The tolls, rates and charges opposite the items undermentioned shall be payable in respect he several matters to which the same refer, and shall not be eu of any wharfage rate or toll if any for the same matters ively prescribed by the Act, viz." Then followed a list of ns and rates thereon varying considerably from those in the th Schedule to the Act, and not containing any rate for is not otherwise enumerated." Evidence was given as to composition and history of the material sought to be charged the rate, and as to the commercial meaning of " guano." nature of that evidence is sufficiently indicated in the judgnt hereunder. e learned Judge found that "guano" was originally the ne given only to the accumulated excrement of sea birds found e dry climate of Peru, and rich in phosphoric acid and en; that the meaning of the word had become extended so to include bird excreta from which substantially all the en had been washed, but not so as to include a substance ch had originally been coral but which had been phosphatized by contact with bird excreta; and that the substance in respect if which the rates were claimed was phosphatized coral and not excreta.

text: He therefore gave judgment c plaintiffs for £300 with costs. On appeal to the Full Court [Holroyd, a Beckett and 1 JJ.] this judgment was affirmed. As to the meaning of s¢ of the Melbourne Harbour Trust Act 1890, and of the Se Schedule thereto, Holroyd J. said :—" In order to make any s¢ of the Seventh Schedule to the Melbourne Harbour Trust 1890, I am driven to construe the words 'not otherwise enm ated' as meaning not enumerated in any other of the grou articles contained in the said Schedule, there being a diff amount of rate assigned to each of such groups; and I therefore with the other members of the Court that the ra the items in the Seventh Schedule are to be charged Commissioners deal with those rates respectively, and t alteration in the amount to be charged upon any one or n items would not affect the rate upon any other item unalte the regulations." The defendant Company now appealed to the High Co Coldham and Cussen, for the appellant.
